entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that manages user, session, and agent states through a token-efficient ADD-only extraction algorithm. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval system—fusing semantic, keyword, and entity matching—to ensure high-precision recall across its managed cloud and self-hosted Apache-2.0 versions.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'brain' for Hermes Agent utilizes a self-wiring knowledge graph with typed entity references to organize information. It excels at complex relationship mapping by combining vector retrieval with graph-based ranking, all accessible via a native MCP server integration.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight focuses on the long-term learning cycle by implementing specific retain, recall, and reflect workflows through a simple two-line LLM wrapper. It is designed for developers who need automatic memory management that works across diverse providers, including local models via Ollama.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho serves as a robust library for building stateful agents, maintaining persistent records of evolving entities and user traits through natural language querying. It provides the foundational infrastructure for sophisticated interaction history, supporting both Python and TypeScript ecosystems.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses five specialized agent roles to create an iterative self-improvement loop for complex tasks. It generates structured playbooks and persistent knowledge traces, offering native integration for Hermes Agent and other MCP-compatible clients.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM provides lossless context management by using a hierarchical DAG structure to compact conversations without ever deleting raw message history. Its SQLite-backed engine includes specialized tools for agents to grep, describe, and expand compacted context as needed.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a dedicated self-hosted backend for the Honcho memory library, specifically tailored for private cross-session persistence within Hermes Agent. It includes automated setup scripts for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis to facilitate easy deployment with any OpenAI-compatible provider.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a triple-threat retrieval strategy—BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs—to provide secure memory via MCP server and agent plugins.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: ContextPilot optimizes agent performance by reducing prompt tokens by up to 36% through intelligent context deduplication and prefix sharing. It significantly boosts prefill speeds for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, making it ideal for high-throughput inference environments.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format, enabling zero-cost local storage for multi-agent systems. It mimics human cognition using ACT-R activation to strengthen frequently used facts while allowing stale information to decay naturally.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep functions as a reflective memory system that indexes code, documents, and media using OCR and automatic summarization. It builds navigable, bidirectional relationships between data points using edge tags, accessible to agents through MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency SQLite backend designed for sub-millisecond latency in local Hermes Agent environments. It features native vector and full-text search alongside built-in episodic consolidation cycles to keep agent memory organized and performant.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this anticipatory memory system prefetches relevant context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. It relies on a local-first architecture using SQLite-vec and node-llama-cpp to provide low-latency context injection via MCP.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST AI RMF. It equips Hermes with specialized security domains, ensuring the agent operates with industry-standard defensive and offensive knowledge.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw enables collective intelligence by automating skill deduplication and quality improvements across different agent frameworks and devices. It allows Hermes to evolve its capabilities over time by sharing and refining learned skills within user groups.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells using a comprehensive word replacement table. It ensures Hermes produces more natural, human-like prose by performing multi-pass audits to catch recycled transitions.
entries.wondelai/skills: This is a modular, cross-platform library that provides Hermes with ready-made frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It supports easy installation via a dedicated CLI, making it a versatile marketplace for expanding agent utility.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Implementing the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI, this project uses progressive disclosure to reduce token usage when managing skill metadata. It offers Hermes a type-safe way to handle both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams, including UML and system architectures, from simple natural language descriptions. It features an iterative feedback loop that enables the agent to self-check and refine visual exports in multiple formats.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and identify structural conservation laws within complex codebases. It enhances Hermes's reasoning by generating constraint reports that highlight potential analysis blind spots and limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This project integrates Hermes with the Chainlink network, enabling standardized interactions with CCIP, data feeds, and smart contracts. It provides the necessary tools for cross-chain monitoring and local simulations within the agentskills.io specification.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave together prose, diagrams, and source code into synchronized documents. It includes a tangler to extract runnable files and ensures that any edits to the source code are automatically reflected back in the documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory and a pipeline for training its own replacement models through decision-quality tagging. It focuses on long-term work retention and the automated extraction of fine-tuning data from agent sessions.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills with support for recursive dependency resolution and integrated commerce. It allows for the discovery and installation of skills using both traditional and blockchain-based payment flows.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. They enable the agent to handle structured JSON prompting and specific hex color requirements for high-quality visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill watches active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills for the agent. It streamlines development by generating both the necessary documentation and Python scaffolds for newly identified patterns.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro acts as a harness for long-running tasks, using structured missions and milestones to manage agent state outside of chat history. It allows Hermes to launch fresh sessions with markdown handoff briefs, ensuring continuity across complex projects.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to execute complex, multi-step tasks autonomously with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It provides real-time progress tracking and safe rollback options for long-running agentic operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token consumption while maintaining connections to multiple servers. It provides Hermes with a persistent daemon for inspecting tool metadata and retrieving schemas on demand.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures Hermes has access to verifiable audit trails with line-level citations for the codebases it interacts with.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community marketplace that features automated security scanning and an agent-to-agent feedback system for skill reliability. It provides a centralized hub for searching, browsing, and one-click installation of trusted agent capabilities.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, integrating with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading. It allows Hermes to utilize progressive disclosure and automatic skill registration within C# environments.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill connects Hermes to Nextcloud services, allowing the agent to manage files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV. It includes a guided setup for secure HTTPS communication using Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of Nix packages and flakes that enable instant, reproducible execution of AI coding agents. It features automated daily updates to ensure users always have access to the latest CLI-based agentic development tools.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level security layers. It offers dynamic policy presets to strictly control network and filesystem access while supporting persistent memory and over 40 built-in skills.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible, system-level deployment that supports both Nix-managed and interactive CLI skill installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent provides a GUI-driven experience with 100 built-in tools and support for local models via LM Studio. It functions as a portable automation engine that integrates TTS and ComfyUI without requiring Docker or complex environment setups.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent for sandboxed execution. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces from a single web application. It enables hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and provides enterprise-grade MCP access for external AI integrations.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: This deployment path configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers. It is optimized for production environments requiring autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This solution deploys a Hermes agent stack via Portainer, featuring a ttyd-powered web terminal for browser-based interaction. It simplifies lifecycle management by integrating directly with Git repositories and automating image builds through GitHub Actions.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It integrates a human-in-the-loop review system called Aegis, allowing users to monitor spend and sign off on tasks before execution.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw facilitates the creation of autonomous agent swarms through hierarchical delegation and visual organizational charts. It supports over 23 LLM providers and features durable memory, enabling agents to learn new skills directly from reviewed conversation histories.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This meta-optimization framework uses an automated outer-loop search to refine harness code for the Hermes Agent. By orchestrating TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations, it identifies performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent and Paperclip, vie for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and provides a real-time dashboard to track task execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains to handle complex software engineering tasks like research and infrastructure. It enforces a strict pipeline architecture with mandatory security audits and quality gates for every code change.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase workflow that enforces architectural integrity through automated graph-diff phase gates.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation tool routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution through isolated workflows. It prioritizes safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits during agent handoffs.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI provides local data visualization for tracking token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding environments. It is essential for Hermes developers who need to monitor resource usage alongside other agents like Claude Code and Gemini.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files. It supports custom ASCII banners and spinner animations, offering a way to create and share unique visual themes while inheriting default system behaviors.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for a native, process-free experience. It eliminates the need for OAuth and external processes while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, this tool exposes web navigation to agents via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features a supervisory UI with annotated checkpoints, allowing developers to audit and supervise Hermes Agent as it performs browser-based tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders. It allows for seamless switching between default and local agents, each with its own project-specific memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that performs deterministic scans of agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs. It helps developers maintain high-quality Hermes implementations by detecting seven specific structural failure modes, including H1-H7 patterns.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It ensures a smooth onboarding process by handling the transfer of complex model configurations and local credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, enabling a zero-cost agent environment in minutes.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This interface provides a mobile-responsive, three-panel layout that mirrors the full Hermes Agent CLI experience in a web browser. It features a unique visual context ring for real-time token monitoring alongside integrated file browsing and navigation.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes across eight channels including Telegram, Discord, and Slack. It includes advanced usage analytics with cost estimation and a web terminal for remote backend management.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace acts as a multi-agent control plane featuring 'Swarm Mode' and role-based dispatching. It integrates a live memory editor and a dedicated marketplace for discovering and configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for managing individual agent profiles with the ability to start, stop, and configure them via a central dashboard. It offers a split-view file editor with syntax highlighting and detailed real-time analytics on model performance and costs.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Built specifically for macOS, this workspace connects directly to Hermes environments via SSH without requiring remote helper services. It provides a native desktop experience for session management, Kanban boards, and remote file editing.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React application brings a terminal-style chat experience to the Telegram Mini App ecosystem. It supports background agent spawning for up to five concurrent instances and provides real-time monitoring of system hardware metrics.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that enables local model usage via LM Studio without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools, including TTS and ComfyUI, and features a dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers at runtime.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the initial setup of Hermes Agent through a guided installer that resolves local dependencies. It features a unified GUI for managing 16 messaging gateways and includes a SQLite-backed session search for deep conversation history retrieval.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A specialized dashboard focused on process monitoring, service health, and the management of scheduled agent cron jobs. It provides full-text search capabilities for inspecting conversation histories and a dedicated browser for exploring skill sources.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that emphasizes human-in-the-loop interaction through real-time tool approval cards and SSE streaming. It includes an integrated marketplace for MCP servers and isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This lightweight, glassmorphic React application uses a Python proxy server to provide a streamlined interface for self-hosted assistants. It features automated session and transcript repair alongside integrated terminal and file management tools.
